codeReview.summary: The rasterizer adaptation is well-executed. All custom TrueType table parsing (cmap format 4, GPOS pair adjustment, font table headers, TrueTypeFont struct) was removed from truetype.rs (~1200 lines deleted). The scanline rasterizer algorithm (bezier flattening, active edge sweep, coverage map generation, subpixel rendering, FIR filter, stem darkening) is fully preserved in shaping/src/rasterize.rs. New read-fonts-based helpers (font_metrics, glyph_id_for_char, glyph_h_metrics) properly replace the deleted functionality. The GlyphCache in lib.rs was updated to use shaping::rasterize instead of TrueTypeFont, and GLYPH_FIRST/GLYPH_LAST/GLYPH_COUNT constants were replaced with inline values and ASCII_CACHE_COUNT. draw_proportional_string and draw_proportional_string_kerned were deleted. Compositor and core service properly updated to use shaping::rasterize::font_metrics instead of TrueTypeFont::new. Build system correctly adds shaping as a dependency for the drawing library via rustc_rlib_with_search. Tests: ~80 old tests for deleted APIs removed, 8 existing subpixel/oversampled rasterizer tests updated to new API, 5 new glyph-ID-based rasterize tests added. All verification steps pass per handoff.

issues.description: truetype.rs retains shared types (GlyphMetrics, GlyphOutline, GlyphPoint, RasterBuffer) and scaling helpers (scale_fu, scale_fu_ceil, scale_fu_floor) that are now duplicated in shaping/src/rasterize.rs. These types in truetype.rs all have #[allow(dead_code)] because they are no longer used from the drawing library — rasterization now goes through shaping::rasterize. This is technical debt: the drawing library includes dead type definitions. The worker correctly noted this in discoveredIssues.
Redundant cast: `(glyph_id as u16) < num_h_metrics` — glyph_id is already u16. This pattern appears in both glyph_h_metrics() and extract_outline(). Not a bug, just unnecessary.

In extract_outline(), for composite glyphs, nested composites are skipped with a comment 'Nested composites: skip for simplicity'. This means deeply nested composite glyphs (rare but valid in some fonts) won't render. The old custom parser also had the same limitation, so this is not a regression — but worth noting for future variable font work.

The handoff claims 6 new tests, but the diff shows 5 new test functions: rasterize_valid_glyph_produces_coverage, rasterize_notdef_glyph_produces_valid_coverage, rasterize_invalid_glyph_returns_none, rasterize_a_glyph_reasonable_dimensions, rasterize_proportional_font_valid. Minor discrepancy in handoff documentation (count says 6, actual is 5). Not a code issue.

GlyphCache::populate() now allocates a RasterScratch on the stack inside the method. RasterScratch is a large struct (~50KB+ with MAX_SEGMENTS=2048 segments of 16 bytes each + GlyphOutline). This could be a stack overflow risk on bare metal where stack sizes are limited. The old API took scratch as a parameter (heap-allocated by the compositor). However, since populate() is called from the compositor which heap-allocates the GlyphCache, the compositor could still heap-allocate scratch if needed — but the current code puts it on the stack. In practice, the function uses `shaping::rasterize::RasterScratch::zeroed()` which is the shaping version (also large). Worth monitoring if stack overflows occur at boot.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added Rust formatting convention section to CLAUDE.md (rustfmt config, file layout convention). This is a positive contribution documenting project standards. However, this change is unrelated to the rasterizer adaptation feature — it appears to be scope creep from a prior session that got committed alongside this feature's changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git diff 0f31aec~1 0f31aec -- CLAUDE.md shows +31 lines adding 'Rust Formatting Convention (MANDATORY)' section. Not mentioned in feature description or expectedBehavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The build system change introduced a new function `rustc_rlib_with_search` in build.rs to handle libraries with additional search paths (for Cargo-managed transitive dependencies). This pattern will likely be needed for future libraries that depend on Cargo-managed crates. AGENTS.md mentions the build system approach but not this specific function.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/build.rs: new rustc_rlib_with_search() function at line ~303, used by drawing library compilation to find shaping's transitive deps.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed skill procedure (TDD, tests first, implementation, test run, build). Skill feedback notes that visual verification via QEMU was correctly skipped for this library-level change, aligning with the skill's guidance that QEMU verification is mandatory only for display pipeline changes. Worker's suggestion about documenting rustc_rlib_with_search in the skill is valid — future features may need this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, no deviations. suggestedChanges includes guidance for QEMU skip and build.rs pattern documentation.

summary: PASS. The rasterizer adaptation is a clean, well-executed migration from the custom TrueType parser to read-fonts. All custom parsing code (~1200 lines) was removed as required. The scanline rasterizer algorithm is preserved intact in the shaping library. New read-fonts-based helper functions properly replace deleted functionality. GlyphCache, compositor, and core service are updated to use the new API. Five new tests and eight updated tests cover the validation requirements. The main technical debt is duplicated type definitions between truetype.rs and shaping/src/rasterize.rs, which the worker correctly identified. No blocking issues found.
